title: verbascum nigrum var. parisiense
intro: Verbascum nigrum var. parisiense, also known as Common Mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that grows up to 2 meters in height, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Verbascum nigrum var. parisiense include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and relieve menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical use, and its dried leaves and flowers can also be used in herbal infusions and decoctions.
